channel.title: Raleigh NC Parks and Recreation News
channel.description: Latest news from Raleigh Parks and Recreation

item.title: Rumblin' Tumblers Class at Brier Creek (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
In this class, children will learn the basic building blocks of tumbling, including forward rolls, backward rolls, cartwheels, and handstands. Our experienced instructors will break down these skills into easy-to-follow steps, ensuring your child's safety while they learn. Through a series of playful activities and games, we'll help kids improve their coordination and balance.



Tumbling is an excellent way to boost self-confidence. Children will overcome challenges, achieve goals, and celebrate their accomplishments, fostering a sense of pride in their abilities. We encourage kids to express themselves through movement and creativity. Safety is our top priority, and our dedicated instructors are trained to ensure a safe environment.



Location: Brier Creek Community Center
Date: Mondays
Ages 4-5, 5:15 - 6 p.m.
Ages 6-10, 6:15 - 7 p.m.
Cost: $41
Register on RecLink

item.title: Marsh Creek Park Partners with Raleigh City Farm on New Urban Farm (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
Through this collaboration, the City has strengthened its commitment to community enrichment, agricultural education, and improved food access—further advancing Raleigh Parks’ vision of creating healthy, vibrant spaces for all.



The transformation of 1.5 acres at Marsh Creek Park into a thriving urban farm hub is already making a meaningful impact. The site now features a central growing space and teaching garden, building upon the park’s existing raised beds, pollinator gardens, and edible landscaping. Additional enhancements will include an outdoor prep and teaching area, supporting a wide range of educational and community programs.



Raleigh City Farm, a nonprofit urban farm founded in 2011 in downtown Raleigh, has brought its expertise and mission to “connect and nourish the community through regenerative agriculture” to this new site. The partnership reflects a shared commitment to addressing food insecurity, strengthening local food systems, and creating hands-on learning opportunities in Raleigh’s public spaces.



The recent Gather and Grow: Urban Farm Open House at Marsh Creek Park was a tremendous success, drawing strong community participation and enthusiasm. Attendees toured the site, connected with staff from both organizations, and took home fresh vegetables. The community’s excitement was a great sign for future success.



“It’s my pleasure to welcome the team from Raleigh City Farm into our family at Marsh Creek Park,” said Jamie Quinn. “They bring an incredible amount of passion, knowledge, and experience and we are so thrilled to see how they will help us to cultivate more than just produce – this urban farm partnership will empower people with the skills to grow their own food, increase access to healthy meals, teach people cook, and bring our community closer through shared purpose and passion.”

item.title: MELT Into Summer Wellness at Lake Johnson Park (Apr 9, 2026)
item.description:
Hosted at the Thomas G. Crowder Woodland Center, these guided sessions offer a simple yet powerful way to support the body’s natural healing processes. The MELT Method is a gentle, hands-off bodywork technique that focuses on the body’s connective tissue, or fascia, to help reduce chronic pain, improve alignment, and restore balance to the nervous system. 

Participants will be guided through easy-to-follow compression and lengthening techniques using a soft foam roller and specialized MELT therapy balls. All equipment is provided, making it easy for beginners to participate.



Classes will be offered on select dates throughout the summer:



Participants should be able to get on and off the floor and are encouraged to bring a yoga mat if they have one. Advanced registration is required for all classes. To register, visit RecLink at RecLink.RaleighNC.gov and search “MELT,” or register in person at any community center.



For additional information, contact Jenny at jenny@foodfityoga.com



For those interested in trying the technique before registering, a free drop-in demonstration will be held on Sunday, April 26, from 1 – 3 p.m. at the Lake Johnson Waterfront Center. Attendees can observe, ask questions, or experience the method firsthand.



Location: Thomas G. Crowder Woodland Center
Date: Varies, starting May 14
Time: Varies
Age: 18 and up
Cost: $22 Resident/$37 Non-Resident
Register on RecLink

item.title: Understanding Streaming: A Beginner’s Guide to Cutting the Cord (Apr 1, 2026)
item.description:
Learn the steps some people are taking to cut the cord. But at the end of the day are you really “Cutting the Cord” or simply paying more for television on a different platform?



What you will learn:



Location: Anne Gordon Center for Active Adults
Date: Wednesdays, April 15 and April 22
Time: 2 - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: 55 and older
Cost: $12
Register on RecLink

item.title: Expressive Motion: Modern and Creative Dance (Apr 1, 2026)
item.description:
You’ll be guided by an instructor to try different movements, focusing on smooth flow, and you’ll learn short dance sequences. This class offers a welcoming space for community members to move, connect, and express themselves while improving strength, flexibility, coordination, and creativity. 

No prior dance experience required.



Location: Jaycee Community Center
Date: Tuesdays, April 21 and April 28
Time: 6:45 - 7:45 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: $10 Drop In
Register on RecLink

item.title: Cat Swim Lessons (Apr 1, 2026)
item.description:
While we’d love to see our feline friends mastering the backstroke, our pools are still reserved for humans (and maybe the occasional rubber duck 🦆).



We hope this gave you a smile and a laugh! At City of Raleigh Parks, Recreation and Cultural Resources, we’re all about creating fun, memorable experiences—both in and out of the water.



Looking for real swim lessons? We’ve got plenty of options for all ages and skill levels (no whiskers required). Learn more about swim programs.



Thanks for playing along, and happy April Fools’ Day!

item.title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Black Joy in the Springtime (Mar 31, 2026)
item.description:
African American households embraced music as a powerful means of self-expression.  Through gospel, jazz, blues, and classical music, African Americans found a way to share their stories, joys, and sorrows. 'Black Joy in the Springtime' beautifully highlights this rich musical heritage at one of Raleigh's historic African American homes. Enjoy a delightful musical performance by Shenette Swann.



Location: Pope House Museum
Date: Saturday, May 9
Time: 2 - 3 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Big Sweep at Lake Wheeler Park (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Thank You to our Volunteers



News Published 3/24/2026 Updated 3/24/2026



This week at Lake Wheeler, we were fortunate to have over a dozen volunteers through the Big Sweep program come and pick up trash!



This program is a Wake County initiative in cooperation with the City of Raleigh to help keep our Parks and Green Spaces clean. Our volunteers cleared 30 pounds of small trash that really requires a slow walk through to find.



Thank you so much to all our amazing Volunteers!



Lake Wheeler Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Hold the Door Mentorship Program (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Chapman will host a special workshop for male youth ages 12-18 on Sunday, April 19, from 2 to 4 p.m. This workshop will introduce the Hold the Door Mentorship Program and its mission to support and empower young men in the community. 



The event will also feature a keynote address from LeVelle Moton, bringing inspiration and insight to attendees. This event marks the official kickoff of this impactful new program, aimed at creating opportunities, guidance, and mentorship for youth in the community.



Location: John P. “Top” Greene African American Cultural Center
Date: Sunday, April 19
Time: 2 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 12 - 18
Cost: Free
No Registration is Required

item.title: Roberts Park Spring Celebration! (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Bring the whole family and enjoy an egg-citing day filled with music, snacks, crafts, games, community resources, and an egg hunt. Don't miss your chance to meet Ms. Bunny and snap some sweet photos!



Date: Saturday, April 4
Time: 11 a.m. - 3 p.m.
Ages: 1 - 12
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Food Vendor Program Expansion (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 3/24/2026 Updated 4/8/2026



Adam Forman
adam.forman@raleighnc.gov 



Abbotts Creek Park
Anderson Point Park
Baileywick Park
Buffaloe Road Athletic Park
John Chavis Memorial Park
Lake Johnson Park
Millbrook Exchange Park
Neuse River Greenway Trail
Shelley Lake Park
Walnut Creek Wetland Park
Laurel Hills Park



Apr 7, 2026



Feb 17, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Celebrate Infertility Awareness Week (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Infertility Awareness Week is observed once a year. This is designed for those who have experienced infertility, those currently navigating the journey, child-free by choice, and anyone who has supported a loved one through it.

Take time to slow down, connect, and find a sense of calm in a supportive environment. 



This inclusive meetup welcomes anyone affected by infertility, navigating it, or child-free by choice. It’s a space to connect, share, and find support in community. Light snacks and small activities for children will be provided. Join us for comfort, conversation, and connection and don’t forget to wear orange in support of the week.

Time: 5:30 - 7 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: A Tree’s Journey: In Celebration of Earth Day (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Participants will read My Tree and Me: A Book of Seasons, reflect on a meaningful tree through drawing and sharing, and embody the strength of a tree through playful movement. 

The class concludes with a peaceful forest visualization meditation, helping children feel calm, grounded, and steady like a tree through every season. Guardians are welcome to drop off their child for this class. Please pre-register so we can plan accordingly.



Location: Kiwanis Community Center
Date: Tuesday, April 21
Time: 5:15 - 6:45 p.m.
Ages: 5 - 10
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Let Us Grow: Come Out to Celebrate Earth Day (Mar 24, 2026)
item.description:
Learn all about the parts of a plant, enjoy a story, and get hands-on by planting seeds to take home. A healthy snack with fresh veggies and fruit will be provided. Weather permitting, the program will take place at the outdoor shelter. Please pre-register so we can plan accordingly.



Location: Kiwanis Community Center
Date: Wednesday, April 22
Time: 10 - 11 a.m.
Ages: 0 - 5
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: 8th Annual Mud Day Event! (Mar 23, 2026)
item.description:
Learn about local environmental groups in our area while participating in the fun activities they offer, visit local food trucks, and of course, slide in a giant mud pit! Additional details coming soon!



Saturday, Aug. 15
From 10 a.m. to 1 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Location: 950 Peterson Street, Raleigh, NC 27610

item.title: Top Greene’s “Adornment of Grace” Hat Show Contest (Mar 23, 2026)
item.description:
Please join us for The Hat Show Contest, a celebratory event honoring creativity, style, and cultural expression on Sunday, May 17 from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m.



We will feature a special guest, Dr. Donna Corbett, and The Mary Corbett Hat Collection will showcase a timeless collection of her mother’s vintage hats, highlighting the enduring elegance and cultural influence of classic millinery. 

From refined silhouettes to bold statements, this presentation honors the legacy of style passed from one generation to the next, and Erica Kendrick, a ceramic artist, sculptor, and educator



Everyone who attends will be entered into the contest and have the opportunity to showcase their hat for a chance to win a FREE 3-hour rental at Top Greene.



Bring your confidence, your culture, and your crown — because the hat makes the statement. Come out, show love, and help us make this a night to remember!

item.title: Xtreme STEAM Sports (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
You and your STEAM Team will have a ball learning the science behind the world’s most popular sports while engineering your way through a stadium full of sporting events. From football to NASCAR, from baseball to bobsledding, from tennis to basketball, to the Olympic Games.



Participants should bring snacks, a reusable water bottle, and arrive wearing closed-toed shoes. Program hours are 1 - 4 p.m.



Location: Sanderford Road Neighborhood Center
Date: Monday, July 6 - Friday, July 10
Time: 1 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 7 - 10
Cost: $150
Register on RecLink

item.title: More Trails Coming to Forest Ridge Park (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh City Council authorized the City to accept a $350,000 Great Trails State Program grant to support the Forest Ridge Park Mountain Bike Trail Extension Project.



The project will add 7.3 miles of new trail, expanding mountain biking opportunities in Raleigh. Construction is currently scheduled for completion in 2028.



Learn more and follow project updates on the project webpage

item.title: Food Vending Survey (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
We’re always looking for ways to improve the food vending experience in our parks, and your input is an important part of that process.



We’ve created a short survey to gather feedback on your experience vending in Raleigh Parks. Your responses will help us better understand what’s working well and where we can make improvements.



The survey should only take a few minutes to complete. Thank you in advance for sharing your thoughts and helping us enhance our programs and services.



Take the Survey  



Questions, please email adam.forman@raleighnc.gov

item.title: Lost Raleigh: Exploring the Oak City's Architectural History Book Launch (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description: This book is a comprehensive look at the city’s cultural and architectural past and a plea to protect and preserve the landmarks that make our cities unique, connecting us to our shared history. Grab your copy, get it personally signed, and walk away with an exclusive, limited edition bookmark absolutely free.

item.title: Life Crafts Camp (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
This half-day camp will teach hands-on life skills, including sewing, cooking, and gardening. These skills will support you through life! Participants should bring a snack and a water bottle. Advance registration is required.



Location: Peach Road Park
Date: Friday, May 1
Time: 8 a.m. - noon
Ages: 7 - 11
Cost: $35 Residents / $50 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: Raleigh Parks Spring Events and Programs (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 4/7/2026 Updated 4/7/2026

Time: 10 a.m. - 2 p.m.

Learn more about the Springtime Wreath Workshop



2100 Old North Carolina 98 HWY, Wake Forest, NC 27587
Date: Saturday, April 18
Time: 9 - 11 a.m. /  1 - 3 p.m.
Ages: 14 and older
Cost: Varies ($12 - $41)
Learn more about the Ready to Ride this Spring

Learn more about the Annual Heirloom Plant Sale

Learn more about the Garden Play

Learn more about Raleigh Earth Day



Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Raleigh Earth Day (Apr 7, 2026)
item.description:
This family-friendly event brings together community members, local organizations, and city departments for an evening dedicated to environmental stewardship, sustainability, and connection—all in one of Raleigh’s most beautiful public spaces.

Explore earth-conscious vendors, enjoy live performances and hands-on activities, celebrate local environmental leaders, and end the night with an inspirational film on the big screen under the stars.

The event emphasizes low-waste practices and practical ways to care for our planet every day. 



Help support Dix Park event staff, ensuring everyone has a fun and safe experience, and the park is clean and green after the event!
Sign up and learn more about volunteering



When: Friday, April 24, 2026, 5 - 10 p.m.
Where: Harvey Hill at Dix Park - Cranmer Dr, Raleigh, NC 27603

item.title: LeadHERship Basketball Clinic (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
Worthdale Community Center



News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/31/2026



Worthdale Community Center, in partnership with Paradigm Sports Academy Inc., is proud to host the first session of LeadHERship Basketball Clinic!



This all-girls clinic is all about leveling up, building skills, boosting confidence, and deepening your understanding of the game in a fun, supportive environment. Whether you're sharpening your fundamentals or learning new moves, this is your time to shine!



Location: Worthdale Community Center 
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: 13 - 18 
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink



Worthdale Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Breakfast with the Bunny and Egg Hunt (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
Saturday, April 4 at Biltmore Hills



News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/31/2026



You will even have the opportunity to take a picture with the Biltmore Bunny. Breakfast is at 10 a.m., and the Egg Hunt will start at 11:30 a.m. This event is both indoor and outdoor.



Time: 10 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $5 per person
Register on RecLink



Time: 11:30 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Pre-registration is not required



Biltmore Hills Park
2615 Fitzgerald Drive
Raleigh, NC 27610



919-996-6895



Biltmore Hills Park



Feb 17, 2026



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Share Your Voice in the Biltmore Hills Master Plan Update (Mar 30, 2026)
item.description:
News Published 3/30/2026 Updated 3/30/2026



Community members are encouraged to participate either in person or online. Public input will help inform the design and shape the long-term vision for the expansion and enhancement of Biltmore Hills Park.



A Design Alternates Public Meeting will be held on Tuesday, April 14, from 4 to 6 p.m. at the Biltmore Hills Community Center. This family-friendly public workshop will give participants the opportunity to learn about the project, compare the design alternates developed for the Biltmore Hills Park Expansion, and share feedback on specific park amenities and layouts that matter most to them.



For those unable to attend in person, an online survey will be available from April 6 through April 20, providing another way to share input and participate in the planning process.



Accommodation and translation services are available upon request. Please email BiltmoreHillsUpdate@publicinput.com for assistance.



More information about the project, engagement opportunities, and online participation is available on the project engagement portal.



Location: Biltmore Hills Community Center
Date: Tuesday, April 14
Time: 4 - 6 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free



April 14, 2026 4:00PM



BiltmoreHillsUpdate@PublicInput.com



Biltmore Hills Park



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Many Ways. One Greenway. (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Are you the cyclist? The walker/runner? The nature observer? The outdoor adventurer? Pick the one that feels like you, and let us know how you use the greenway.



From April to May, you'll see friendly reminders along the trails about etiquette on our greenways, and you may run into our Raleigh Parks Ambassadors with some fun giveaway items and activities!



Take the quiz about how you use the greenway to receive a discount on your next Raleigh Parks program.

item.title: Free Suicide Prevention Training (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Raleigh Parks Health and Wellness, in partnership with the QPR Institute will host a free Question, Persuade, Refer (QPR) Training on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, from 6 to 8 p.m. at Jaycee Park Community Center (2405 Wade Ave, Raleigh, NC 27607).



This event is open to all North Carolina residents and is designed to provide suicide prevention knowledge and skills. 



Just as people trained in CPR and the Heimlich Maneuver help save thousands of lives each year, people trained in QPR learn how to recognize the warning signs of a suicide crisis and how to question, persuade, and refer someone to help.



Date: Wednesday, May 13
Time: 6 - 8 p.m. 
Location: Jaycee Community Center (2405 Wade Ave, Raleigh, NC 27607)
Registration: QPR Training- Jaycee  



Key Information: 



 Content Notice: This training includes discussion about suicide and mental health. These topics may be distressing for some individuals. If you or someone you know is in crisis, support is available by calling or texting 988, the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline.

item.title: Exploring the Oak City's Architectural History (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description: This book is a comprehensive look at the city’s cultural and architectural past and a plea to protect and preserve the landmarks that make our cities unique, connecting us to our shared history. Grab your copy, get it personally signed, and walk away with an exclusive, limited edition bookmark absolutely free.

item.title: Hiking for Beginners (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Learn the basics of hiking on a 3-mile hike with an experienced instructor. You'll learn how to plan a hike, what to bring, and what to wear.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Date: Sunday, May 3
Time: 9 - 11 a.m.
Age: 14 and older
Cost: $6
Register on RecLink

item.title: Annual International Children's Day (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Meet these incredible North Carolina creators, hear them read from their award-winning books, and get your copies personalized



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, May 30 
Time: 10 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Introduction to Camping (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
This program will provide a wealth of knowledge for new campers or those planning and leading group trips. We will walk you through the basics of camping in four instruction sessions. The sessions will cover finding a place to camp, navigating online reservation systems, campsite rules, and what to do when you get there. 



You will learn about camp cooking, outdoor ethics, first aid, camp safety, and what to do in an emergency. After the sessions, our instructors will lead a camping trip to practice your skills at Durant Nature Preserve, Aug. 29 - 30. You must attend 3 of the 4 instruction sessions to participate in the campout. Please contact Forest Ridge Park for more information.



Instruction sessions: First Thursday of every month (5/7, 6/4, 7/2, 8/6), 5:30 - 7 p.m.
Campout at Durant: Aug. 29 - 30
Ages: Sessions -18 and older/ Campout - Family/All ages
Cost: $65 resident /$80 non-resident
Register on RecLink

item.title: Calligraphy Class (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
We have a lot of fun classes with engaging and knowledgeable instructors. Come learn calligraphy for adults ages 50 and up. Linda Torrena will teach participants how to use different brushstrokes to create beautiful lettering. This workshop is every other Wednesday for six weeks starting May 13.



Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Every other Wednesday, May 13 - July 22
Time:  10:30 a.m. - 12:30 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $ 29
Register on RecLink

item.title: Pullen Park Electric Train Celebration Weekend (Apr 6, 2026)
item.description:
Friday April, 17 - Sunday, April 19



News Published 4/6/2026 Updated 4/6/2026



Be among the first to take a ride on the eco-friendly engine and participate in celebratory activities all weekend. Listen to live music, make a train craft, learn about railway safety, or join a story time in the caboose!



Check out the available ticketing packages on RecLink to join the fun! Full schedule of events coming soon.



Dates: Friday April, 17 - Sunday, April 19
Time: 10 a.m. - 8 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: $6 - $10
Get Tickets on RecLink



April 17, 2026 10:00AM



Pullen Park Amusements



This feedback widget is not intended for customer service issues.



This feedback is reviewed monthly to help us improve our site. For immediate customer service please refer to our staff directory.

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture with Cheetie Kumar (Mar 13, 2026)
item.description:
She has been profiled in The New York Times, Southern Living, and The Wall Street Journal, and many other national publications.



Join us for an in-depth Q&A on Cheetie’s career, food advocacy work, and upcoming projects in the Raleigh restaurant scene.

item.title: Springtime Wreath Workshop (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
You will receive step-by-step guidance on how to arrange and attach decorations to the base, and learn techniques to achieve a visually appealing and balanced design. You will leave the class with a one-of-a-kind wreath!



You are encouraged to bring your own decorations (3-4), ribbon, and greenery if you have a certain theme in mind. Otherwise, we will have some decorations available. All other supplies and tools will be provided.



Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Friday, April 17
Time: 1 - 4 p.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $31
Register on RecLink

item.title: Participate in the City Nature Challenge! (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
Visit our preserves, parks, community centers, and greenways to find plants, animals (including insects), fungi, and other living things. Download the free iNaturalist app in the Apple or Android App Stores to submit photos of the things you see. Instructions on How to Make an Observation.



You can participate anywhere in Raleigh parks, preserves, and greenways, our community centers, downtown, or anywhere in the world! By documenting your observations with iNaturalist, you will learn about the living things around you and connect with a community of other people who value learning about nature. You will also help guide natural resources management decisions and contribute to scientific studies locally and internationally, while competing to find more species than other cities.



Location: Anywhere (Raleigh parks, preserves, and greenways, our community centers, downtown, or anywhere in the world)
Dates: Friday, April 24 - Monday, April 27
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Instructions on How to Make an Observation

item.title: Wow Mom: Gifts for Mother's Day (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
In our two hours we will create a set of candles, a paper flower bouquet, and a little card to tell mom just how amazing she is. We encourage assistance, but adults aren’t required to register unless they will be participating themselves. Registration is required , and this is not a drop-in event. The crafts will take 2 hours.

Location: Lake Lynn Community Center
Date: Saturday, May 9
Time: 10 a.m. - Noon
Age: 5 and older
Cost: $15
Register on RecLink

item.title: City of Oaks Open Pétanque Tournament (Mar 20, 2026)
item.description:
Register your team of two or three players for three one-hour rounds in the morning, a break for lunch, and then single elimination in the afternoon. Bring your enthusiasm and get ready to point and shoot!



Location: Pullen Park - Upper Shelter Area
Date: Saturday, April 25
Check-In: 8:30 a.m.
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: Free
Registration Form

item.title: Youth Summer Basketball Registration (Mar 27, 2026)
item.description:
The City of Raleigh Athletics Division will be offering outdoor youth basketball for ages 9-12 (as of August 31, 2025). League age groups, 9-10 and 11-12, will be offered as an outdoor basketball league at the Baileywick Park Outdoor Pavilion.  



Open registration will be held from April 1-14. Interested athletes may register online or at any City Parks community center during the registration period.  The Athletics Program will provide the general equipment for practices and games, as well as team uniforms.  



For more information about youth basketball, please contact Athletics by visiting the Athletics website, calling 919-996-6836, or emailing athletics@raleighnc.gov. 



Registration: April 1 - 14
Ages: 9 to 14 years old (League age as of Aug. 31, 2025)
Fee: $65 for Residents / $80 for Non-Residents
Register on RecLink beginning April 1

item.title: Adult Basketball Registration (Mar 27, 2026)
item.description:
You may register online or in person at the Athletics Division office located at 2401 Wade Avenue, Raleigh, NC 27607. In-person registration hours are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5:15 p.m.



If you are not currently part of a team, you are encouraged to join the league by being added to the Free Agent list. To be placed on the list, please contact City of Raleigh Athletics. For additional information or assistance with registration, call 919-996-6836 or email athletics@raleighnc.gov. 



The Adult Basketball League offers a great opportunity for you to stay active, build camaraderie, and enjoy friendly competition.



Locations: Abbotts Creek, Carolina Pines, Halifax, Jaycee, Laurel Hills, Lions, and Marsh Creek
Game Days: Monday - Thursday
Ages: 18 and up
Cost: $450 Team
Register on RecLink

item.title: Planting Power Exhibition Closing Reception (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Planting Power: 100 Years of the Raleigh Garden Club explores a century of history of this major civic organization. Since its founding in March 1925, the Raleigh Garden Club has played a vital role in shaping the city’s green spaces. Its founding mission to create “a garden with a city in the heart of it” has resulted in a century of notable plantings and projects. Visit the exhibit and learn the story of how individual volunteers, in what began as an all-woman garden club, had a major impact on Raleigh's appearance for one hundred years and counting. 



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Wednesday, April 15
Time: 10 - 11 a.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free

item.title: Did You Know Raleigh Parks Uses Prescribed Burns? (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
This video provides an inside look at how and why trained staff use controlled, carefully planned burns as a land management tool. Prescribed burns help reduce the buildup of dry vegetation, lower the risk of uncontrolled wildfires, and promote the growth of native plants.



“Prescribed burns are a critical part of how we care for our natural areas,” said Brian Smith, Natural Resources Manager. “They allow us to safely manage vegetation, support wildlife habitats, and keep our parks resilient for the future.”



In addition to improving safety, prescribed burns play a key role in restoring natural ecosystems. Many native plant species depend on fire to thrive, and these burns help maintain the balance needed for diverse and healthy habitats.



The video also emphasizes the planning and expertise involved in each burn. Controlled burns are led by trained and experienced Raleigh Parks staff and supported by trained professionals from agencies such as the NC Forest Service. Strict safety guidelines are used, including the monitoring of weather conditions, to minimize impacts from smoke. As Raleigh continues to grow, proactive land management practices like prescribed burns help ensure that parks remain safe, sustainable, and enjoyable for generations to come.

item.title: Little Sprouts Event at Lake Lynn (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Designed especially for our curious Pre-K learners, this event is a celebration of the wonders of Spring. Families will enjoy hands-on activities, seasonal crafts, and a nature scavenger hunt while supplies last.



Partnering with Leesville Library, there will also be a library craft and two story times at 10:30 a.m. and 11:30 a.m. Registration is encouraged , but not required.



Location: Lake Lynn Park
Date: Monday, May 11
Time:  10 a.m. - noon
Ages: 3-5 years old
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Soccer Teacher Workday Camp (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
In the morning, they’ll spend two hours learning and practicing skills with coaches, then enjoy friendly soccer games, team challenges, and traditional camp activities. It’s a full day of fun for beginners or soccer fans alike!



Time: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m.
Ages: 6 - 11
Cost: Resident: $80 (Non-Resident: $95)
Register on RecLink

item.title: Lunchbox Lecture with Julia Einstein (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description: A local painter known for delving into the study of floral history to fill her garden and studio with inspiration. Julia will walk us through the growing season as inspiration for her large flower icon paintings!

item.title: Family Fun Day Squirrels! (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Come to the City of Raleigh museum and celebrate all things related to our favorite woodland creature. Make squirrel headbands, search for acorns, and even take a picture with our very own mascot, Sammy the Squirrel!



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Saturday, April 11
Time: 11 a.m. - 2 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
No Registration Required

item.title: Drawing in the Galleries (Mar 19, 2026)
item.description:
Well, this may be your best chance! Join us for a still-life drawing session using our own objects as reference materials.



We will provide the lights and the action. You will need to bring any sketching supplies. Please no paints or liquid materials. This is a ticketed event for 21 and over. Learn more and get tickets !



Location: City of Raleigh Museum
Date: Friday, Apr 17
Time: 7 - 9 p.m.
Ages: 21 and older
Cost: $20
Learn More and Get Tickets

item.title: Partial Reopening of Pullen Amusements Begins April 1 (Mar 26, 2026)
item.description:
Beginning April 1, Pullen Park Amusement rides, attractions, playground, and amenities will reopen while work continues on the Lake Howell shoreline improvement project. We’re excited to welcome visitors back to many of the park’s favorite attractions this spring and summer.



Most amusements and the main park areas will be open and operating. Visitors will see construction activity near the lake, and some brief, early morning and weekday closures may be necessary for materials delivery.



Know Before You Go



What Remains Closed



We appreciate your understanding as we make these necessary improvements to help keep Pullen Park safe, accessible, and enjoyable for years to come. We encourage you to explore other Raleigh Parks while improvements are underway. 



For updates, visit our project webpage or follow Raleigh Parks on social media.

item.title: Memoir and Personal Essay Writing Workshop (Mar 26, 2026)
item.description:
It can be funny, touching, or life-changing. Whether you know what you'd like to write about or you need help finding it, this class will have various tools and exercises to help you along the way. 

At the end of 6 weeks, all students will have a 2-3 page story that is personal to them, and a wonderful treasure to share with loved ones.

Location: Five Points Center for Active Adults
Date: Wednesdays, July 8 - Aug. 12 
Time: 11 a.m. - Noon
Ages: 18 and older
Cost: $18 Residents / $33 Non-residents
Register on RecLink

item.title: More than $1 Million Secured to Restore Rocky Branch at Dix Park (Apr 2, 2026)
item.description:
Rocky Branch Creek is on its way to becoming a nature escape in the heart of Raleigh.  



Congresswoman Deborah Ross joined Mayor Janet Cowell and City leaders to announce $1,092,000 in federal funding for the Rocky Branch Restoration.  



The project opens up more public space for residents to connect with nature. Visitors will explore restored creek banks, spot native plants and wildlife, and enjoy new pathways, boardwalks, and shaded seating areas. Once complete, Dix Park will have a connected green space between Downtown Raleigh and Gipson Play Plaza. 



These improvements aren’t just for people to enjoy. The project helps prevent flooding by giving the creek room to hold stormwater. It will also restore plants and habitat to improve water quality for Dix Park and downstream neighbors.

item.title: Green Road Family Sneaker Ball (Mar 25, 2026)
item.description:
This family-friendly event is sure to knock your socks off! Come dressed in semi-formal attire and your coolest kicks.



This event will have an awesome DJ, games, arts and crafts, and food trucks. Let us see your sneaker game while you dance the night away.



Location: Green Road Community Center
Date: Friday, May 1
Time: 6:30 - 9 p.m.
Ages: All
Cost: Free
Register on RecLink

item.title: Level Up! Mountain Bike Basics (Mar 17, 2026)
item.description:
Our certified instructors will guide you through the fundamentals of mountain biking. Learn and practice riding techniques, body position, braking, and shifting. 

The course will also cover trail etiquette and local mountain biking resources. We'll wrap up with a group trail ride, putting your new skills to use. Bikes and helmets are provided. 

Successful completion of the course qualifies you to participate in the Bike Loaner Program at Forest Ridge Park. Preregistration is required.



Location: Forest Ridge Park
Dates: Saturdays - April 18, May 16, Aug. 22
Time: 9-11 a.m. 
Ages: 14 and up
Cost: $26 Resident / $41 Non-residents
Register on RecLink
